title: Crunchy Broil Ukrainian Shredded Monterey Jack Cheese Pastry
description: Funky, crunchy brunch-mood Ukrainian dish featuring shredded Monterey Jack cheese, broil, and Ayurvedic-sattvic-friendly ingredients served on a slate-tile vessel.
instructions: Combine the gluten-free flour, rye flour, egg, and salt in a large mixing bowl.
Gradually add the warm water, mixing until a dough forms.
Knead the dough on a floured surface for 5 minutes, until smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 6 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Flatten each ball of dough into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
Place a tablespoon of shredded cheese in the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough over the cheese, forming a triangle or square shape, and press the edges together to seal the pastry.
Brush the tops of the pastries with a little water and sprinkle with sesame seeds and chopped fresh dill, if using.
Place the pastries on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and broil for 3-4 minutes on each side, or until golden brown.
Serve warm on a slate-tile vessel, garnished with additional chopped fresh dill and sesame seeds if desired.
Repeat with the remaining dough and cheese to make additional pastries.
Store leftover pastries in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 3 days.
To serve, simply reheat in the oven or toaster oven until crispy and warm.
Serve with a dollop of yogurt or sour cream for a satisfying snack or light meal.
Optional: sprinkle with additional shredded cheese and chopped fresh herbs before serving for extra flavor and color.
